hypothesis: If an XGBoost model is trained to predict the difference between GFN1-xTB and DFT (PBE/HSE06) band gaps of MOFs using selected stoichiometric, RAC, and APRDF descriptors, then its cross-validated error on DFT band gaps will be lower than that of direct-learning XGBoost and fine-tuned MOFTransformer/CGCNN models because leveraging the semi-empirical baseline allows the model to learn compact corrections to systematic errors.
